name: State of Israel Declared Independence
summary: David Ben-Gurion proclaimed the establishment of the State of Israel, marking the end of the British Mandate and the beginning of a new nation in the Middle East.

name: First Arab-Israeli War Begins
summary: Neighboring Arab states invaded the newly declared State of Israel, initiating a regional conflict that would reshape the Middle East.

name: Universal Declaration of Human Rights Adopted
summary: The United Nations General Assembly adopted the Universal Declaration of Human Rights, establishing a global standard for human rights protections.

name: Berlin Blockade Begins
summary: The Soviet Union blocked all ground access to West Berlin, triggering a major Cold War crisis and leading to the Berlin Airlift.

name: Gandhi Assassinated
summary: Mahatma Gandhi, leader of India’s nonviolent independence movement, was assassinated, shocking the nation and the world.

name: National Party Wins South African Election (Start of Apartheid)
summary: The National Party came to power in South Africa and began implementing apartheid, a system of institutionalized racial segregation.

name: World Health Organization Founded
summary: The World Health Organization was established as a specialized agency of the United Nations to promote global public health.

name: Burma Gains Independence
summary: Burma (now Myanmar) gained independence from British colonial rule, becoming a sovereign nation.

name: Czechoslovak Communist Coup
summary: The Communist Party seized control of the government in Czechoslovakia, aligning the country with the Soviet bloc during the early Cold War.
